Issues to be addressed at hearing: 1) Not all heirs have submitted Probate Code Section 13101 affidavits. 2) The notice of hearing filed October 20, 2025, shows an incorrect hearing date. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TRACEY ANN JONES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/30/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proof of service of notice of the FOURTH amended petition e-filed on 10/24/25 has been filed. (2) The signatures and verification, allegedly of this FOURTH AMENDED PETITION, are identical to those on the initial petition filed on 10/24/24 (and on every subsequent "amended" petition) and, in fact, still has that initial date, 10/22/24, not the current date. (3) The verification, also being a copy of the one attached to the original petition, verifies the wrong pleading. (4) This fourth amended petition still says that the administrator sold the decedent's residence. THERE WAS NO REAL PROPERTY on the inventory. (5) Schedule 7 (proposed distribution) shows the wrong amount of legal fees. (6) It appears from schedule 4 (disbursements) that funds were held by the attorneys, not by the administrator...? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/20/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE IRVING B. SACKS REVOCABLE TRUST DTD OCTOBER 17, 2013
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Approving Settlement Agreement And Distribution Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Notice is defective. The proposed order is incomplete. (A petitioner does not give notice to him/herself. [Probate Code Section 1201.]) Papers may not be faxed or emailed unless directed by the Examiner. [LRSF 14. B.] Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RICHARD WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (2) A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] (3) Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) requires notice if an heir or beneficiary is or has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. (Not the decedent.) (4) The petition caption does not disclose that costs are to be reimbursed to the administrator. [CRC 7.102.] (5) There is no description of what made up the $3,500 in costs reportedly advanced by the administrator. Unless the two heirs waive their right to know what constituted the costs, they must be set forth in detail. (6) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] The initial petition never set forth the identities of the decedent's parents and their dates of death. Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/25. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] In addition to the requirements of CRC 2.111 and 7.102, all pleadings must also show the name and representative capacity, if any, of the party for whom the attorney is appearing, e.g., John Jones, Executor. "Petitioner" or "Respondent" alone is not sufficient. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CORNELIA B. HOPPE 2021 TRUST DATED MARCH 4, 2021
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/22/25. Prior to a full review of the petition, the following threshold issue must be addressed by a verified declaration: the proper venue for a trust petition is the county where the principal place of administration of the trust is located [Probate Code 17005(a)(1)]. The Probate Code 16061.7 Notice attached to the petition indicates that the principal place of administration is in Petaluma; thus, San Francisco does not appear to be the proper venue. All documents to be filed by 12/8/25.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YVONNE MA LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/28/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) No courtesy copies of any responsive papers were received by the assigned examiner by the 11/5/25 deadline. The copy of the bank statements e-filed on 11/6/25 do not indicate that Yvonne Ma Lee was the owner of the East West Bank account. In fact, there does not appear to be any name on that ledger. If bank statements are not available, a letter signed by an officer of the bank must be filed. If courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues are not received by the assigned examiner by 1/14/26, the hearing will be continued again. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
